decisions.title: Use a global CLI with data-only target repositories
decisions.context: Copied templates made every target repository carry viewer code, schemas, local tools, package files, and dependencies.
decisions.decision: Architext 1.0 serves viewer assets, schemas, validation, and starter templates from the installed package. Target repositories commit only data, metadata, and optional instructions.
consequences: Global CLI availability becomes the primary operating assumption

decisions.title: Preserve architecture facts during migration
decisions.context: Old installs may contain valuable project-owned data mixed with copied package-owned implementation files.
decisions.decision: Migration preserves docs/architext/data/*.json, removes copied package-owned files, updates metadata, and corrects managed agent instruction sections.
consequences: Architecture facts are not rewritten silently
Dry-run output must show destructive removals
Instruction updates are part of migration correctness

decisions.title: Manage one Architext instruction section
decisions.context: Agents need current rules, but project-specific instructions outside Architext must remain authoritative.
decisions.decision: The CLI replaces or appends a single section headed 'Architext Architecture Documentation' in AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md.
consequences: Old copied-template instructions can be corrected automatically
Highly unusual Markdown layouts may need manual review

decisions.title: Validate with package-owned schemas
decisions.context: Committing schemas into every target repository made schema upgrades a repo-by-repo file-copy problem.
decisions.decision: Schemas ship with the Architext package and are used by architext validate [path].
consequences: Validation behavior tracks the installed CLI version
Schema migrations must be explicit when data shape changes
Target repos no longer review schema diffs unless Architext itself changes

decisions.title: Ship the distribution change as a major version
decisions.context: The target repository footprint and upgrade behavior are incompatible with the pre-1.0 copied-template model.
decisions.decision: Release the global CLI/data-only model as Architext 1.0.0.
consequences: Users receive a clear breaking-change boundary
Existing copied installs are migration inputs
Documentation can stop describing copied templates as the normal path

decisions.title: Separate domain policy from CLI, browser, and rendering adapters
decisions.context: The global CLI and viewer had working behavior concentrated in large driver files, making routing, lifecycle, validation, and presentation changes harder to reason about.
decisions.decision: Move command parsing/presentation, doctor repair derivation, architecture reference validation, C4 quality policy, viewer preference/data adapters, view selection policy, diagram planning state, and routing primitives into focused modules.
consequences: Routing fixes can target geometry, ports, route indexing, and rendering separately
CLI lifecycle changes can share doctor/sync policy
React remains responsible for rendering instead of owning architecture facts
